title: #incidents: 6865432109
preview:
incident-bot: :rotating_light: Incident 6865432109 - Dedicated pool us-west-2 experiencing p99 latency spike (2.8s) and streaming stalls. Autoscaler warmup delays detected. Paging oncall + eng-sre. Dash: https://dashboards.redwood/incident/6865432109

Alex Cheng: ack, I'm on call. seeing spike on the graphs now. p95 ~800ms, p99 ~2.8s, backlog queue depth jumped to ~12k. :grimacing:

Jordan Wells: we've got 3 paying tenants reporting degraded streaming (long stalls then reconnect). CS notified. Trying to gather which customers exactly now.

Priya Natarajan: logging in to control plane now. first pass: what changed in last deploy? any model variant rollouts or kernel changes?

Miguel Torres: nothing rolling in prod in last 2 hours AFAIK. runtime telemetry shows a surge of warmup failures, handshake times spiking. suspect warmup slot starvation in us-west pool.

Samir Patel: checking autoscaler logs and pool state. pulling current desired vs running:```json
{"pool":"us-west-2-dedicated","desired_replicas":24,"ready_replicas":18,"warming_replicas":3,"pending_warmups":9,"queue_depth":12134}```

Priya Natarajan: pending_warmups 9 while ready < desired -> autoscaler triggered but warmup latency high. Any kv-cache coldpath or heavy quantized loads?

Miguel Torres: runtime logs show repeated handshake timeouts (~12s) for warmup tasks. excerpt:```
[2026-03-15T10:12:03Z] warmup: slot_alloc failed handshake_timeout_ms=12000 model_variant=redwood-quant-v2
[2026-03-15T10:12:04Z] warmup: retrying slot_alloc
```

Alex Cheng: could be kernel selection regression on the new runtime build. Sam, can you check node GPU load and if any nodes stuck in init state?

Samir Patel: running node check:```
redwoodctl nodes list --pool us-west-2-dedicated
# output (trimmed)
node-42 gpu_util=98% status=ready
node-43 gpu_util=95% status=ready
node-47 gpu_util=3%  status=initializing (warmup hung)
node-49 gpu_util=2%  status=initializing (warmup hung)
```
Looks like 2 nodes stuck initializing, not accepting warmups. Investigating kube events.

Leah Kim: customers on the hotshot tier will notice first. Can we route their traffic to a fallback region or a smaller model variant temporarily?

Alex Cheng: immediate mitigations: 1) scale reserve capacity up (manual), 2) enable fallback to eu-west for non-data-residency tenants, 3) reduce concurrency for streaming to shed load. I'm going to manually bump pool desired to 30 to force more provisioning.

Priya Natarajan: manual bump ok, but warmup still needs to finish. Suggest we also toggle warmup_preload=false for a minute so we don't block on full model load for some requests. That would trade latency for correctness but reduce handshake pressure.

Miguel Torres: agree. toggling warmup_preload for hotshot routing paths. issuing command:```bash
redwoodctl config set --pool us-west-2-dedicated warmup_preload=false --reason "live mitigation 6865432109"
```

Samir Patel: bumped desired_replicas to 30. provisioning started. kube events show nodes spinning up, but a lot depends on image pull and kernel module init times (we're seeing a slow init on a subset).

Jordan Wells: notifying affected customers: "We're investigating increased streaming latency in US-West. We've routed traffic to fallback capacity and are working on mitigation. Will update in 15m."

incident-bot: Notification: oncall escalation to eng-sre@redwood for p99 breach. Runbook link: https://runbooks.redwood/pools/warmup-starvation

Alex Cheng: quick test of failover routing: I toggled a small percentage of traffic to eu-west and p99 for that shard dropped to 580ms. Good signal.

Priya Natarajan: current telemetry after mitigations: p95=420ms, p99=1.1s, queue_depth=4.2k, pending_warmups=3. Improvement but still above SLO at p99.

Miguel Torres: starting to roll a temporary kernel fallback on 4 nodes (previous stable kernel) to see if handshake timeouts stop. Will coordinate with infra.

Samir Patel: kernel rollback staged to node-47,node-49,node-51,node-52. Applying one-by-one to avoid mass restart. If rollback clears init hang, warmup should catch up.

Leah Kim: product wants a customer impact summary when we have a stable snapshot. Also flagging this for the weekly reliability review.

Alex Cheng: status update: kernel rollback completed on node-47 and node-49. warmup failures for those nodes dropped, pending_warmups->1, queue_depth->1800. p99 trending toward 750ms.

Jordan Wells: CS: two customers reported timeouts earlier, both on hotshot; we've confirmed retries for streaming succeeded after failover. No financial data impacted.

Priya Natarajan: next steps: 1) keep fallback routing until pool warmups finish, 2) collect warmup handshake traces and attach to incident, 3) run postmortem to determine why image init/kmod path regressed.

Miguel Torres: grabbing traces now, will upload to the incident timeline. Also scheduling a canary roll of the new runtime build into a small pool to reproduce locally.

Alex Cheng: handing over to eng-sre for post-incident follow-up. If anything regresses ping this thread. Thanks all. :heartpulse:

Leah Kim: noted — product will draft comms for impacted customers and a short status update for slack channels once postmortem is ready.

incident-bot: Incident 6865432109 - mitigation in progress. Current severity: Sev2. Open tasks: attach traces, postmortem owner, update runbook with warmup rollback play.

Alex Cheng: postmortem owner: Priya. I'll close paging once p99 < 1s for 15m sustained.

Priya Natarajan: taking postmortem ownership. ETA for initial writeup 24h. Closing updates for now — leaving thread open for any followups.

title: #architecture: 4891234567
preview:
Samir Rao: FYI a customer hit repeated defer loops when the routing policy kept sending retries to the low-cost pool. Saw client timeouts. Need a quick design decision on preventing re-defer loops. :frowning:

Priya Natarajan: Can you share the trace? My initial thought: we need a per-decision marker so retries know a defer already happened.

Samir Rao: Trace: ```[2026-03-20T13:10:02Z] decision id=9f3de defer_to=pool-low-cost
[2026-03-20T13:10:06Z] client_timeout id=9f3de
[2026-03-20T13:10:06Z] retry parent=abc123 -> decision id=8aa21 defer_to=pool-low-cost
```

Maya Lin: Two policy options: (A) Central policy adds a "defer_window_ms" and runtime honors it; (B) runtime tracks ephemeral token and forbids re-defer for same parent_request_id.

Priya Natarajan: I prefer runtime enforcement with central defaults. Faster, fewer RPCs. But we need token signing for private installs and a telemetry flag to validate.

Samir Rao: Agreed. Also add a fallback strategy enum: [immediate-fallback, hedge-first, retry-later]. Default could be immediate-fallback when parent ID seen.

Maya Lin: Telemetry fields to add: decision_id, parent_request_id, defer_window_ms, defer_hit (bool), token_sig_ok (bool), latency_buckets=[0-5,5-20,20-100,100-500,500+].

Priya Natarajan: Implementation plan: runtime adds small signed token {decision_id, exp_ms}; central controller ships default defer_window and max_window. Canary 5% traffic, monitor client timeout rate and tenant cost delta.

infra-bot: Deploy queued: sidecar/0.12.3 -> canary(5%) on 2026-03-22T01:00:00Z. Release notes: defer token + telemetry + defer_window support.

Samir Rao: Action items: @Priya build token HMAC library, @Maya update policy schema + telemetry docs, I'll prepare canary dashboards and incident replay.

Priya Natarajan: Will PR HMAC lib later today.

Maya Lin: Updating docs and CI tests now.

Samir Rao: Thanks — regroup after 48h metrics.

title: #partnerships: 3479000000
preview:
Evan Cruz: Quick thread for EdgeScale x Redwood mini-lab (conference booth + livestream) on 2024-06-11. Need AV checklist, accessibility captions, on-site contact, and battery/shipping restrictions.
Priya Natarajan: I'll own captions/CART vendor. Recommend Captionly (we used them at last summit). Need transcript SLA of <=2 min.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): Logistics: we have 4 demo rigs but 2 have battery packs with lithium cells -> customs + airline paperwork required. Can EdgeScale ops ship freight?
Hannah Cho: Ops note: batteries trigger hazardous paperwork. If partner ships, we need HS codes + MSDS by 2024-05-20. Otherwise we can swap to demo units with external AC only.
Evan Cruz: Could we do a hybrid approach: 2 battery units on-site, 2 hosted fallbacks? That reduces risk.
Priya Natarajan: Accessibility run-of-show suggestion: add 3x 30s caption checks and 1 pause for alt-text descriptions for demo visuals.
ops-bot: Created checklist: "Demo AV + Accessibility" and invited Evan, Priya, Hannah, Leo.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): On-site lead is Raj Patel -> +1 415-555-0199. He can clear customs on pickup. I'll DM his passport number to Hannah offline.
Hannah Cho: Shipping cutoff for booth arrival (on-site consolidation) is 2024-06-04. Last pickup to meet that = 2024-05-31.
Evan Cruz: Run-of-show (draft):
```
09:30 - Booth open / warmup
10:00 - Stream on / host welcome (Evan)
10:05 - Partner overview (Leo)
10:12 - Demo A: Live inference (EdgeScale hw) - captions ON
10:25 - Demo B: Hosted fallback + policy switch (Priya)
10:35 - 10:40 - Accessibility pause / image descriptions
10:40 - Q&A (moderated)
10:55 - CTA / workshop signup
```
Priya Natarajan: AV needs: 2x HDMI capture (1080p30 ok), 1x camera for close-up, wired ethernet (no Wi-Fi dependency), 2x headset mics. Also a small lav for partner speaker.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): We'll bring an EdgeScale HDMI breakout and a USB-C power brick. Any venue power strips?
Hannah Cho: Venue supplies standard strips but bring an extra 2m IEC extension. Also bring a travel UPS for the host laptop.
Evan Cruz: Recording: volunteer for single-cam recording? I can handle recording + upload to shared drive. Need someone to clip the 30s highlight for socials.
Priya Natarajan: I'll take clipping. Also will coordinate with Captionly for pre-event test at 2024-06-07 11:00 PT. Caption vendor needs the RTMP stream key by 2024-06-05.
ops-bot: Reminder set: "Submit RTMP key to Captionly" on 2024-06-05.
Hannah Cho: Swag plan: small tote + sticker sheet. Return logistics: include prepaid return label if attendee signs up for post-event kit. Who owns returns?
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): EdgeScale can handle returns for demo loaners. For swag returns we prefer Redwood manage.
Evan Cruz: Action items:
- Leo: send HS codes + MSDS for battery units (due 2024-05-20)
- Hannah: confirm freight cutoff + pickup window (due 2024-05-22)
- Priya: Captionly booking + test stream (due 2024-06-07)
- Evan: upload recording + create highlight clip (due 2024-06-12)
- All: speaker bio (100-150 words) + headshot (png) to marketing by 2024-05-24
Priya Natarajan: Registration cap recommendation: 300 live seats and unlimited on-demand. Keeps booth throughput manageable.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): Quick fallback plan: if battery units are held at customs, switch to hosted Redwood nodes and show the automap route in console. I can prepare a 2-min recorded fallback clip as Plan B.
Hannah Cho: For customs-friendly shipping, include description: "demo units - no sale value - batteries declared" and add return label. Also include a short packing checklist on the outside of each crate.
Evan Cruz: Great. Also remind speakers to enable live captions on their laptops as a backup (macOS auto-caption or Chrome live captions).
Priya Natarajan: Noted. I'll send a speaker accessibility brief with tips (alt text, speaking cadence, live captions) by 2024-05-28.
Evan Cruz: Thanks team. I'll update the shared doc with these actions and ping again after freight confirmation. :rocket:

title: #marketing: 2667001234
preview:
Rae Chen: Heads-up: want to run a compact 'Welcome & Webinar' nurture for new newsletter signups next week. Primary goal: drive webinar RSVPs and surface preference signals. Keep it lightweight (2 emails). Thoughts? :sparkles:

Omar Ali: Good idea. Recommend segment by signup intent tag (doc_downloaded, demo_requested, general). That will give cleaner signal on which creative works.

Priya Natarajan: Copy plan: Email 1 = welcome + single CTA to RSVP (highlight one benchmark stat). Email 2 (48h later for opens only) = quick how-to + reminder. Two subject variants for Email 1: "Welcome + Benchmarks" vs "Start in 5 minutes". A/B 50/50 within each intent segment.

Cam (Analytics): Measurement: primary KPI = RSVP rate @ D7, uplift vs control. Please add utm_campaign=news-welcome-2667, intent_tag, subject_var. Also keep a small 5% control that gets a plain text note.

Devon (Ops): Throttle plan: seeds first, then 40/min for first hour then ramp. Add jitter +/-15m. If hard_bounce > 1% pause.

Rae Chen: Draft handshake body (short): "Hey {{first_name}} — welcome! See a quick snapshot of our latest inference benchmarks and join a 20-min live Q&A. RSVP -> [link]. Prefer product tips? Click here to switch." Keep <120 words.

Omar Ali: For the RSVP landing page, prefill email + intent_tag and add a hidden field for lead_score. Also tag RSVP events for downstream scoring.

Priya Natarajan: I'll prepare the two subject variants and the follow-up micro-email tonight. Also add an alternate CTA for 'Snooze' to reduce complaints.

Cam (Analytics): I'll create the dashboard filters and the uplift calc. Null hypothesis: subject A drives >=1.2ppt higher RSVP than B. We'll run test for 7 days.

Devon (Ops): I'll run seed sends Tue 10am, create cohorts Wed, and enable jitter on Fri send. Posting send script in #eng-ops when ready.

Rae Chen: Action items:
- Priya: deliver subject variants + follow-up copy
- Devon: cohorts, seeds, throttle + jitter
- Cam: dashboard + tagging spec
- Omar: landing prefill + RSVP event hook

Rae Chen: Can QA be tomorrow EOD? If green, schedule sends for Fri 11:00 PST. Thanks team! :raised_hands:

title: #marketing: 2612345678
preview:
Maya Rivera: Quick idea — want to run a 'warm pulse' weekend send for trial signups this Friday. Short handshake email + 3-day micro-nurture. Goal: convert high-intent -> webinar RSVP, others -> standard nurture. Thoughts on staggering windows to avoid queue spikes? :wave:

Sam Chen: Love it. My rec is 3 cohorts by activity signals: (A) product tour / API used (high intent), (B) signed up but no usage (warm), (C) partner/marketing signups (cold). Stagger sends by cohort to smooth throughput and give each cohort time to react. :+1:

Sam Chen: Proposed windows (rough):
```
Cohort A: Fri 11:00-11:30 PST
Cohort B: Fri 12:00-13:00 PST
Cohort C: Sat 09:00-10:30 PST
```

Sofia Park: Quick company update — pleased to announce Rajiv Malhotra is joining Redwood Inference as Director, Inference Reliability, starting Apr 14. He'll lead site reliability for inference, observability, and reliability-focused model rollouts. :tada:

Evan Carter: big win. timeline for backfills? we'll need a senior SRE and a monitoring PM on the roadmap.

title: #people-ops: 2491203456
preview:
Aisha Patel: Quick announce — we’re running a 12-week Platform Rotation Fellow pilot to accelerate cross-team leadership development and cover an immediate gap in Dedicated Services. The goal: one senior engineer or manager rotates into the Senior Manager, Dedicated Services role for 12 weeks while Recruiting runs a full backfill hire. Details below.

Aisha Patel: Why — we need (1) uninterrupted ownership for the dedicated product workstream, (2) a development pathway for senior ICs to get manager experience, and (3) time to run a deliberate external search.

Aisha Patel: Timeline — rotation starts May 2, onboarding week Apr 29–May 3. Internal EOIs due Apr 12, hiring req live Apr 5, final candidate selected by Apr 19.

Aisha Patel: Scope — the fellow will inherit the Dedicated roadmap squad, be the escalation for latency/cost incidents, own sprint planning and cross-team coordination, and lead the canary rollouts for the May capacity change. This is a temporary manager seat (12 weeks) — comp band and headcount do not change.

Recruiting: We’ll post the backfill req in Greenhouse by Apr 5. We have two internal potentials in the funnel already and will supplement with targeted reach-outs to senior candidates who’ve worked in infra+serving.

Tomás Rivera: Love this program. I’ll push it to eng-leads and flag folks who’ve expressed interest in management. :+1:

Sofia Ramirez: Quick Q — is the fellow required to be in SF/NY, or is this open to remote/hybrid folks?

Aisha Patel: Remote ok — we’ll support hybrid across NA/EU. There will be a few fixed meeting times (weekly leadership sync, on-call handoff) that the fellow must attend live.

Priya Natarajan: Hiring ops note — People Ops will run a short calibration panel for internal candidates to keep this fair. We’ll also confirm comp neutrality so there’s no salary reset.

Sofia Ramirez: I’m interested — DMing you now, Aisha. Curious about the expected time commitment outside of normal sprint cadence.

Aisha Patel: Thanks Sofia — roughly +1.5–2 hrs/day the first two weeks for handoffs, then ~1 hr/day ongoing for org syncs, plus normal sprint duties. We’ll work with your manager on capacity.

Recruiting: Heads up to hiring managers — please update interview rubric to reflect manager responsibilities (1:1 coaching, stakeholder mgmt, incident leadership). We’ll share a short candidate brief template.

Tomás Rivera: I'll cover the canary rollout owner during the overlap period if needed. Happy to pair with the fellow for the first release window. :rocket:

People Ops Bot: Reminder — create rotation approval doc in /company/rotations and file the temporary role in Greenhouse by Apr 5.

Aisha Patel: Action items: (1) People Ops to post internal EOI form (by EOD), (2) Recruiting to create req (by Apr 5), (3) Managers to flag potential candidates, (4) Program leads to add a 30/60/90 for the fellow so objectives are clear.

Tomás Rivera: ✅ will add the 30/60/90 template to the program doc.

Sofia Ramirez: Thanks — excited about this pilot. :party:

title: #partnerships: 2190004321
preview:
Maya Patel: Heads up — Vanguard Compute just returned a countersigned NDA and want to kick off a short early-access bench/ops loop next week. They requested a 1-node sandbox, telemetry access, and a short press embargo.

Diego Ruiz: Great. Do we have a data transfer plan? S3 or SFTP? Also need to confirm marketplace packaging won't block timeline.

Maya Patel: They prefer SFTP for initial drops, will open an S3 endpoint later. They also want a 30-day embargo on any external announcements. @Priya Natarajan can you handle the addendum?

Priya Natarajan: On it. I'll push the NDA addendum today and aim for countersign by EOD tomorrow. Need the Vanguard contact (email) to cc legal.

Connor Lee: For the bench plan, propose we focus on: rwkv-30b, llama2-13b, and a quantized-mid model (q4_8). Test matrix: batch sizes [1,8,32], seq lengths [512,2048], measure p50/p90/p99, throughput and power draw. Also a 2-hour thermal soak run.

Alex Kim: Telemetry: Vanguard will expose GPU power and thermal sensors. We'll ingest into redwood.telemetry.v1. Minimal schema below — we'll need a mapping step on our side:

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-25T16:01:23Z",
  "gpu_id": "gpu-0",
  "power_watts": 345.2,
  "temp_c": 78.1,
  "kv_cache_hit": 0.987
}
```

Connor Lee: Also want network hop counts and PCIe utilization if available. We will correlate with p99 spikes for regression analysis.

Maya Patel: Ops notes: Vanguard can grant lab access Tue-Fri 09:00-17:00 PT next week and only one node initially. We need to order runs to get the most informative data.

Diego Ruiz: Proposed run order: 
1) Baseline perf rwkv-30b (batch=1, seq=512)
2) Quantized run q4_8 (batch=8, seq=512)
3) Long-seq latency (seq=2048, batch=1)
4) Thermal stress (2hr sustained load)

Priya Natarajan: NDA addendum request: add joint-PR clause — no press/release without mutual signoff; 30-day embargo after shared results. I'll add liability caps aligned with our standard EAP terms.

Maya Patel: Owners: Legal=Priya, Bench infra & scripts=Connor, Telemetry ingestion=Alex, Partner ops liaison=Diego. I'll coordinate daily check-ins. :white_check_mark:

partner-bot: Calendar invite created — "Vanguard bench kickoff" on Wed Mar 25 2026 16:00 PT. Join: https://meet.google.com/xxx (invite sent to participants).

Connor Lee: Quick bench script snippet for infra team:

```bash
# run baseline
./bench/run.sh --model rwkv-30b --batch 1 --seq 512 --iters 200 --out results/rwkv-baseline.json
```

Diego Ruiz: SFTP creds exchanged in DM with Maya. We'll also drop a golden prompt set (50 prompts) for quality parity checks.

ProductOps: Marketplace checklist: we need manifest.json, ops runbook, pricing SKU mapping, and a small deployment example. I’ll prep a template for Vanguard to fill.

Alex Kim: Telemetry notes: Vanguard will rotate keys daily — we'll add automation to fetch new creds and rotate our ingestion. Also adding a lightweight parser to validate incoming fields and drop noisy sensors.

Priya Natarajan: Confirmed vendor rule: no customer data in these runs — Vanguard says all workloads will be synthetic. I'll add that to the addendum.

Maya Patel: Good. Diego will post daily bench summaries in #partnerships/results. If any parity regressions show up, Connor please escalate.

Diego Ruiz: First quick summary (posted to results): baseline p99=42ms, throughput=120 tok/s, power idle ~320W; thermal ramp stable over 30m. Attaching logs to the results thread.

Connor Lee: Noted. I'll triage parity diffs and open follow-ups as needed.

Maya Patel: Thanks everyone — solid kickoff. We'll use this thread for ops updates and any blocker triage. :rocket:

preview:

Aisha Patel: Quick company update — we’re consolidating Infra SRE into Platform & creating two new IC manager roles to cover infra reliability and platform integrations. Priya Natarajan: Thanks Aisha. Can you confirm org reporting? will those two managers report to Platform Eng (me) or Runtime?

Diego Morales: Congrats folks. Any change to headcount approvals/process? need to know before we open reqs.

Aisha Patel: Good Q — headcount is pre-approved for the two manager slots and 3 immediate backfills (SRE ICs). PM/TPR approvals are done, budget scoped for Q2 hires. Recruiting will open reqs tomorrow.

HR Bot: Hiring checklist posted: /doc/hiring/infra-platform-checklist.md — includes interview loop, scorecard, and relocation guidance. Reminder: use the new inclusive interview rubric v2.

Screen 4: Behavioral + leadership
```

Priya Natarajan: That looks good. Recruiting — please include a runtime engineer as interviewer for the kernel screen.

preview:
Olivia Park: Quick sync on launch newsletter + 7-day drip for webinar no-shows. Goal: convert product watchers into trial users. Need segment rules, cadence, and warmup plan.
Sanjeev Rao: Agree. We should split no-shows by engagement: watched >30s vs landed only. Different subject/tone.
Tess Morgan: Copy idea for watched >30s: 'You missed the live demo — clips & benchmarks inside' For landed-only: 'See what you missed: 3-minute benchmark highlights'
Priya Natarajan: Segment sizes: watched>30s = 1.8k, landed-only = 3.4k, registrants who converted = 600 (suppress). Also compile a control holdback of 5% per segment for lift measurement.
Micah Lee: Drip cadence proposal: Day0 (send recap & clips), Day2 (short benchmark email + CTA to trial), Day5 (customer story + limited time credit). For no-shows, use lower frequency.
Olivia Park: Deliverability: we need to warm up by sending small batches to high-engagement users first. Plan: send 5% to top-engaged, wait 15m, then 20%, then remainder over 2 hours. Ops rate limit 900/min.
Sanjeev Rao: Also set seed list to major ISPs and internal inboxes to check rendering and spam traps. Alert if bounce rate >2% in first 30m.
Tess Morgan: A/B test subject + CTA placement. Test subject (A): 'Benchmark: 2x latency, 3x cost savings'. (B): 'Redwood 2026: faster inference for production' CTA test: hero CTA vs inline CTA.
Priya Natarajan: UTM params: utm_campaign=launch_march2026_drip, utm_medium=email, utm_source=newsletter. Add campaign tags to CRM so ops can attribute conversions.
Olivia Park: Suppress list: customers with active contract, unsubscribed, bounced last 90d, and people already in paid-trial funnel.
BrandBot: New campaign draft created: https://app.redwood/marketing/campaigns/2024501122 (id 8941) :white_check_mark:
Micah Lee: Sample campaign config for ops (paste into campaign manager):
```
name: "launch_march2026_drip"
segments:
  - webinar_no_show_watched
  - webinar_no_show_landed
exclude:
  - registrants_converted
  - active_customers
holdback: "5%"
send_strategy:
  - stage: "seeding"
    percents: [5,20,75]
    pause_minutes: 15
  - stage: "remainder"
    window_minutes: 120
ab_tests:
  - subject: ["A","B"]
  - cta_position: ["hero","inline"]
utm: "utm_campaign=launch_march2026_drip&utm_source=newsletter"
ops_alerts:
  - bounce_rate_threshold: "2%"
  - deliverability_drop: "15%"
```
Olivia Park: Nice. Last call for copy by 14:00 PT. I'll approve final send plan at 16:00 PT if no red flags. :eyes:

title: #eng-platform: 2005556666
preview:
Alex Kim: quick check — during the canary for /v1/generate the iOS SDK is throwing a JSON parse error when the response omits the optional metadata field vs when it returns \u003cnull\u003e. repro snippet:

Alex Kim: ```bash
curl -i -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  https://api.redwood.ai/v1/generate -d '{"prompt":"hi"}'
```

Alex Kim: example responses we saw:

Alex Kim: ```json
// A: metadata absent
{ "text": "hello" }

// B: metadata explicit null
{ "text": "hello", "metadata": null }
```

Alex Kim: which of these are we guaranteeing? iOS JSONDecoder throws on case B when our SDK models expect a non-optional dict.

Priya Natarajan: good catch. we should decide one canonical behavior. my vote: make metadata optional in the schema (not required) and treat absent OR null as equivalent at the SDK layer. keep the server stable for a canary but don't change semantics immediately. :eyes:

Mateo Ruiz: agree on SDK-side tolerance. for the server contract, proposal: schema v2 explicitly defines metadata as nullable object, but SDKs will normalize null -> {} so callers never see a crash. proposed JSON Schema snippet:

Mateo Ruiz: ```json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "text": { "type": "string" },
    "metadata": { "oneOf": [ { "type": "object" }, { "type": "null" } ] }
  },
  "required": ["text"]
}
```

Mateo Ruiz: this keeps compatibility while being explicit. downside: some clients prefer missing over null for payload size but it's tiny.

Casey Lee: for streaming endpoints we already emit newline-delimited JSON (`application/x-ndjson`). I recommend a strict rule: streaming lines must never contain `metadata: null`; streaming can omit the field when empty. that reduces line length and parsing complexity for streaming parsers.

Priya Natarajan: +1 for streaming omit. we should document that difference: batch (single JSON) allows metadata=null, streaming omits absent fields. add a note in the API reference and the SDK changelogs.

Casey Lee: I'll add a contract test in CI that validates both patterns are accepted by SDKs and that server responses match declared content-type. sample check pseudo:

Casey Lee: ```bash
# contract test: server returns Content-Type header and body conforms to schema v2
curl -s -D- "$ENDPOINT" -o body.json \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $T" -d '{"prompt":"test"}'
# schema validate body.json vs schema_v2.json
```

Jordan White: sounds good. rollout plan? we need to avoid breaking older SDKs.

Mateo Ruiz: plan: deploy schema v2 on a canary region for 24h, run contract tests against canary, then push SDK patch that normalizes null->{} (patch for iOS and JS). after SDKs are out, flip default to v2 globally. fallback: if we see >0.1% parse errors in logs, rollback and open a quick postmortem.

Alex Kim: can you add a quick note to the PR describing normalization so reviewers know why the model changed? i'll ping mobile eng for a quick review.

Priya Natarajan: will do. adding doc TODO: add an explicit sentence in "Response semantics" section about null vs omitted keys and streaming vs batch.

deploy-bot: Scheduled deploy to canary (eu-west-1) at 2026-09-14T22:00:00Z for service api/redwood-generate (sha: d4c3f2a).

Jordan White: tagging mobile @Mateo Ruiz and @Alex Kim for visibility. i'll monitor the error budget dashboard during canary.

Alex Kim: thanks all — i'll follow up after the canary run.

Priya Natarajan: +1 for streaming omit. we should document that difference: batch (single JSON) allows metadata=null, streaming omits absent fields. add a note in the API reference and the SDK changelogs.

Casey Lee: I'll add a contract test in CI that validates both patterns are accepted by SDKs and that server responses match declared content-type. sample check pseudo:

Casey Lee: ```bash
# contract test: server returns Content-Type header and body conforms to schema v2
curl -s -D- "$ENDPOINT" -o body.json \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $T" -d '{"prompt":"test"}'
# schema validate body.json vs schema_v2.json
```

Jordan White: sounds good. rollout plan? we need to avoid breaking older SDKs.

Mateo Ruiz: plan: deploy schema v2 on a canary region for 24h, run contract tests against canary, then push SDK patch that normalizes null->{} (patch for iOS and JS). after SDKs are out, flip default to v2 globally. fallback: if we see >0.1% parse errors in logs, rollback and open a quick postmortem.

Alex Kim: can you add a quick note to the PR describing normalization so reviewers know why the model changed? i'll ping mobile eng for a quick review.

Priya Natarajan: will do. adding doc TODO: add an explicit sentence in "Response semantics" section about null vs omitted keys and streaming vs batch.

Jordan White: tagging mobile @Mateo Ruiz and @Alex Kim for visibility. i'll monitor the error budget dashboard during canary.

Alex Kim: thanks all — i'll follow up after the canary run.

title: #people-ops: 1934556677
preview:
Priya Natarajan: Hi all, quick onboarding/security question — my apartment uses an enterprise WPA2-Enterprise cert profile (workplace Wi-Fi). Do I need to add anything special for Redwood VPN/Okta, or is the usual Okta SSO + Redwood VPN client fine? Also, when does the phishing simulation run and how often should I expect those emails? :thinking:

Ethan Brooks: Welcome Priya! Okta SSO + the Redwood VPN client is sufficient for remote access, you don't need to re-register your home AP. The only thing we require is that your company laptop is enrolled in MDM and FileVault (or BitLocker) is enabled. Phish sims typically run quarterly for most teams; completion of the related micro-training is required if you click a simulated link.

Marta Lopez: Adding on: if you plan to use a personal device for any work, you must request a personal-device exception and enroll it in MDM first. We strongly encourage using the company-issued laptop for dev work. If you want the loaner, reply here and I can kick off provisioning.

onboarding-bot: Reminder: "Security Basics" and "PhishSim Awareness" assigned to @Priya Natarajan. Security Basics due in 14 days. PhishSim micro-course auto-assigned if you interact with the simulation. Learn: https://learn.redwood/sec-basics

Priya Natarajan: Thanks! How do I verify FileVault is on? And for Windows users, BitLocker? I want to confirm before I accept the loaner.

IT Support: Commands to check: macOS:
```
sudo fdesetup status
```
Windows (PowerShell as admin):
```
Get-BitLockerVolume | ft MountPoint, ProtectionStatus
```
If the loaner needs setup, we push the MDM profile and the certs. Once enrolled, you should see the "Redwood MDM" profile in System Preferences / Security & Privacy.

Ethan Brooks: Also a heads-up: phishing sims are low-noise but realistic. If you fail one, the remediation is a short 5-10 minute interactive module. No punitive action, just required completion. If you have concerns about work email forwarding or personal mailing lists, ask People Ops for exemptions.

Priya Natarajan: Good to know. I'll take the company laptop — thanks Marta. Can someone add me to #sec-oncall and #new-joiners? My manager said they'd handle it but want to double-check.

Marta Lopez: Done — added you to both channels and submitted the loaner request (ticket #IT-7421). ETA 48 hours for pickup or courier. Scheduling note: we run a 10-minute security sync for new hires on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 9:30 AM PT. I'll put you on tomorrow's slot.

Priya Natarajan: Perfect, appreciate the quick turnaround :smile:

title: #architecture: 1829998888
preview:
alex chen: quick follow-up from the RFC — i pushed the "cohesion-by-contract" proposal to the doc (link: https://docs.internal/rfcs/cohesion-by-contract). tl;dr: define minimal runtime contracts between hotpath modules and enforce with adapters rather than big-service splits.

priya natarajan: saw it, thanks. can you summarize the big tradeoffs? i'm worried about operational complexity if we add an adapter per module.

alex chen: sure — tradeoff bullets: 1) adapters add a small surface but let us keep services cohesive; 2) faster incremental rollouts vs big-cut rewrites; 3) slight latency cost for adapter hop but much lower overall regression risk.

michael ford: do we have perf numbers for the adapter hop? even 1-2ms on the hotpath adds up.

alex chen: good question. rough bench from spike: adapter baseline ~0.6ms median on p3-like nodes, tail depends on serialization. i put the benchmark tables in the doc. we can do a lighter binary protocol to shave another ~0.2ms.

jordan lee: how are we enforcing contracts? are we adding compile-time checks or runtime validation? both?

alex chen: proposal is a hybrid: compile-time schemas + runtime asserts in dev/staging. production will have a lightweight validation layer that fails fast and streams violation metrics to console.

samir patel: for tech-debt-paydown, can we schedule the adapter migrations as small tickets that each team owns? i'd rather see 2-week windows to remove old code per module.

priya natarajan: agreed on ownership. also we should add a migration checklist: feature-flag, perf-run, canary, kill-legacy. can you add that to the doc, alex?

alex chen: added checklist to the doc under "migration playbook". short version: 1) add adapter + schema 2) run perf + lint 3) feature flag on 10% 4) 72h monitoring 5) ramp 6) remove legacy.

michael ford: what about observability? adapters need consistent tracing and token-level cost visibility.

alex chen: yep — adapters will propagate span context and attach a small metadata tag "contract-v". logs will be tagged, and console will show per-contract latencies.

jordan lee: this helps with decoupling. one more risk: test surface explosion. if every adapter has its own tests, our CI time could blow up.

samir patel: suggestion: adopt contract-level unit tests + a single integration harness that exercises many adapters in parallel. also use selective CI (only run heavy tests on adapter changes).

priya natarajan: sounds reasonable. can we get a phased timeline? i.e., which modules first?

alex chen: suggested phase 0: session-router, kv-cache gateway and request-router adapters (low surface, high coupling wins). phase 1: enrichment adapters, then longer-tail services. timeline in doc approximated at 6 months for core hotpath paydown.

michael ford: :+1: for starting with session-router. will pair on perf tuning.

deploy-bot: Document updated by alex chen (cohesion-by-contract). View at https://docs.internal/rfcs/cohesion-by-contract (v3).

samir patel: i'll open tickets and assign owners next week. want to sync w/ infra on canary windows.

priya natarajan: cool — let's have a 30min sync Wed to lock the phase 0 owners and blockers. please add agenda items to the doc.

alex chen: done. thanks all — i'll post the sync invite and attach short perf slides.

michael ford: thanks, alex. excited to see debt get paid down without a risky big-bang.

title: #eng-platform: 1793287654
preview:
Tara Chen: Thinking about a composite rollout guard: evaluate p50/p95 latency, error rate, and token-cost-delta before ramping traffic. want the config shape + enforcement point opinions.

Jon Park: Do we make it a strict AND (all checks must pass) or a score-based gate? Also: how to surface which check failed in console? :mag:

Tara Chen: Default = strict AND so automatic pause on any hard SLO breach. but allow marking some checks as soft/warn. console should show per-check pass/fail and a small timeline.

Priya Natarajan: We need cost protections. propose token_cost_delta capped per-minute and burst tolerance window otherwise one noisy prompt could spike costs.

Tara Chen: Draft config sketch (plain JSON for UX dev):
```
{"gate":"composite",
 "checks":[
  {"id":"p95_latency","threshold_ms":"1200","severity":"hard"},
  {"id":"error_rate","threshold_pct":"0.5","severity":"hard","exclude_transient":true},
  {"id":"token_cost_delta","threshold_pct":"30","severity":"soft","window_sec":"300","baseline":"per_route"}
 ]
}
```

Jon Park: baseline must be per-route+model-version. and warming: first N minutes of canary should establish baseline (adaptive baseline) to avoid false positives.

Alyssa (infra-bot): guard-schema v2 validated against policy-lint

Alyssa Lin: on implementation: propose a lightweight guard-eval service with an OPA-like policy engine. pipeline: telemetry -> sliding-window aggregates -> guard-eval -> action (noop/pause/alert).

Priya Natarajan: How do we compute token_cost near-RT? billing tables are slow. need estimator from request metadata: tokens_in/out * model_unit_rate from catalog.

Jon Park: agreed. estimator formula: cost = tokens_out * unit_cost + tokens_in * ingress_cost. unit_cost pulled from model catalog (cacheable). acceptable error +/- few % for gating.

Tara Chen: API surface idea:
- Control plane endpoint: POST /v1/rollouts/{rollout_id}/evaluate -> returns PASS/FAIL + details
- Webhook / event stream for pause events
- Console shows live evals, per-check charts, and a manual resume/rollback button

Priya Natarajan: Edge case: transient network errors will inflate error_rate. we should only count persistent failures (e.g., after 1 retry) and have a short hysteresis (N failures in M seconds) before marking the check failed.

Jon Park: add metrics we can dashboards on:
- rollout.guard.evals.total
- rollout.guard.failures.by_check
- rollout.guard.pause.count
- rollout.cost.estimate.delta_pct

Tara Chen: tracing: tag spans with rollout_id, canary_bucket, guard_status so SRE can jump from a trace to the rollout timeline.

Priya Natarajan: Security note: guard configs may reference model_unit_cost. do not leak catalog pricing to tenant-facing logs. cc @security

security: flagged. we'll require RBAC + audit log on guard edits, and redact pricing in any tenant-visible responses.

Jon Park: we should also provide an override path for critical customers: an 'emergency=true' flag that bypasses automatic pause but still records a high-severity audit entry and emits alerts. default off.

Tara Chen: action items:
- Jon: prototype guard-eval API + simple OPA policy module (ETA: 1 week)
- Priya: design near-RT cost estimator and baseline extraction rules + simulation harness (ETA: 3 days)
- Alyssa: add the metrics and draft a dashboard panel for guard health
- Tara: mock console panels and permission model; write RFC

Alyssa (infra-bot): created task RN-2789 for prototype + RN-2790 for dashboard sample

Tara Chen: if no objections i'll open RFC + PRs tomorrow. :thumbsup:

Priya Natarajan: LGTM

Jon Park: LGTM

title: #eng-platform: 1770864420
preview:
Benji Okafor: heads up, hitting a bunch of validation edge cases in the policy engine schema for auto-rollback. want to align on what we reject vs coerce + what the error messages look like (so Console can show something sane)

Jordan Lee: +1. right now it’s failing with generic “invalid config” which is useless. we need field paths + human explanation + bounds.

Ethan Park: can you paste an example of a “bad” config we expect customers/internal services to try?

Benji Okafor: yep, here are a few we saw during the spike (some are from old rollout policy patterns):
```json
{
  "autoRollback": {
    "enabled": true,
    "action": "rollback",
    "signals": {
      "latency_p99": {"burnRate": 0.8, "fastWindow": "1m", "slowWindow": "10m"},
      "error_rate": {"burnRate": 20, "fastWindow": "5m", "slowWindow": "2m"}
    },
    "minRequests": 0,
    "debounce": "0s",
    "holdoff": "90m",
    "suppression": {"mode": "admin_only", "maxDuration": "30d"}
  }
}
```
Issues:
- burnRate < 1 (nonsensical) 
- slowWindow shorter than fastWindow
- minRequests 0
- debounce 0
- holdoff 90m (we said cap at 30m?)
- suppression maxDuration 30d seems too long

Hannah Rivera: Console policy editor also lets ppl type “1minute” or “300” instead of duration strings. we should error clearly, not silently interpret.

Jada Williams: from controller POV: we *must* reject configs that can cause oscillation or infinite rollback/pause loops. especially debounce/holdoff = 0, minRequests = 0, and window ordering.

Sean Gallagher: +1. also burnRate < 1 will basically trigger constantly (or behave weird depending on toolkit). SRE wants hard bounds, not “best effort”.

Allison Grant: naming nit: we should keep the errors consistent across policy engine + Console API. recommend a structured format (even if we return as string) like: `path: message (expected X, got Y)`.

Priya Natarajan: what’s the line between “reject” vs “clamp”? i’d strongly prefer reject for anything safety-related. clamping creates hidden behavior.

Jordan Lee: agree. clamping is tempting but it will surprise service owners. reject w/ explicit bounds so they fix it.

Ethan Park: we should also decide whether some fields are optional and defaulted (e.g., action defaults to pause?), and validation should mention default if missing.

Benji Okafor: current thought:
- missing fields -> default (safe)
- present but invalid -> reject
Except maybe “enabled: false” should allow partially invalid subfields (since it’s not used). But that can be confusing.

Jada Williams: i’d still validate even if disabled. ppl flip enabled later and suddenly it breaks at rollout start. better to catch early.

Hannah Rivera: +1. if user saves in Console and later toggles enabled, we don’t want a surprise 400.

Jordan Lee: ok so always validate. we can let Console show errors inline even when disabled.

Sean Gallagher: re bounds: what are we actually standardizing?
- burn rate: >= 1.0 and <= ? (we’ve talked 1-50?)
- fastWindow: >= 1m?
- slowWindow: >= 10m? and must be >= 3x fastWindow?
- minRequests: >= 200 (or tier-specific?)
- debounce: >= 2m?
- holdoff: between 5m and 30m?

Benji Okafor: PR-28419 had draft bounds (not final):
- burnRate: [1.0, 100.0]
- windows: fastWindow in [1m, 10m], slowWindow in [10m, 2h], and slowWindow >= 3*fastWindow
- minRequests: [200, 100000]
- debounce: [2m, 15m]
- holdoff: [5m, 30m]
- suppression max: [15m, 24h] (admin override can be longer but needs justification?)

Allison Grant: we should include “why” in the error message, not just “out of range”. e.g. “debounce must be >= 2m to prevent flapping”

Jordan Lee: careful on length of error text. Console will show it; keep it short but helpful.

Ethan Park: maybe we return `code` + `message`? but we can’t do nested fields per API? (kidding). for now we can standardize string patterns and later add structured.

Benji Okafor: policy engine API already returns a list of validation errors internally. controller gets them as strings today. Console API likely returns the list too. i can make strings include:
- field path
- short message
- expected
- got
Example:
`autoRollback.signals.latency_p99.burnRate: must be >= 1.0 (got 0.8)`

Hannah Rivera: that format is perfect for inline error under that field.

Jada Williams: can we also include a stable error id so we can search logs / docs? like `RBK_POL_001`.

Jordan Lee: +1, and tests can assert on ids.

Benji Okafor: i can prefix string with id:
`RBK_POL_001 autoRollback.signals.latency_p99.burnRate: must be >= 1.0 (got 0.8)`
and keep the rest human readable.

Sean Gallagher: can we add one for “windows invalid ordering” that explicitly says slow must be >= fast and preferably >= 3x fast? ppl always mess that up.

Benji Okafor: yep.
Potential ids:
- RBK_POL_001 burnRate out of bounds
- RBK_POL_002 invalid duration format
- RBK_POL_003 slowWindow too small / ordering
- RBK_POL_004 minRequests out of bounds
- RBK_POL_005 debounce out of bounds
- RBK_POL_006 holdoff out of bounds
- RBK_POL_007 suppression maxDuration out of bounds
- RBK_POL_008 unknown signal key (typo)

Allison Grant: please include signal names in error for typos. and for unknown fields, do we reject or ignore?

Priya Natarajan: reject unknown fields. ignoring is a footgun.

Jordan Lee: agree. JSON typos should not silently drop a safety signal.

Ethan Park: and include “did you mean” suggestions? maybe too much.

Hannah Rivera: optional but nice. even just “unknown signal ‘latncy_p99’” is ok.

Benji Okafor: i’ll keep it minimal for now.

Connor O'Brien: release-eng angle: can we make the errors actionable enough for “release gate checks”? i want the gate to say *exactly* what to fix if auto-rollback is required before enabling a rollout.

Benji Okafor: yes. gate can fail with the same messages (and we can link docs in the gate output, but probably not in the policy engine error itself).

Jada Williams: one more edge case: if someone sets action=rollback but doesn’t have a baseline version pinned / lastKnownGood missing. that’s not strictly policy config but should still be validated somewhere.

Ethan Park: that should be validated at rollout creation / step transition. policy validation can’t know environment state.

Jordan Lee: +1. policy validation is purely config shape/bounds.

Sean Gallagher: also: durations like “0m” should be rejected with the same duration id.

Benji Okafor: ok.
Action items:
- i’ll update schema validation to always validate regardless of enabled
- add error ids + field paths + expected/got
- hard-reject unknown fields + unknown signal keys
- enforce bounds above (unless SRE wants tweaks)

Allison Grant: please also add one line to docs/standards w/ the error format so teams know what to expect.

Priya Natarajan: ship it behind the flag first; we can tighten bounds later but error ids need to be stable.

Benji Okafor: ack. will follow up with a PR and tag @Jordan @Hannah for review on message wording.

Jordan Lee: :thumbsup: drop a couple test cases with realistic configs so we don’t regress.

Hannah Rivera: and please include at least one multi-error response example so Console can render “5 issues found” nicely.

Benji Okafor: will do.

title: #finance: 1769504321
preview:
Priya Natarajan: Fortem Systems AP says their renewal invoice INV-2027-0223 has a duplicate platform fee line and support tier looks off. Can someone triage? @LenaOrtiz @SamirPatel

Lena Ortiz: Pasted the invoice lines from the portal:
```
Invoice INV-2027-0223
Customer: Fortem Systems LLC
Period: 2027-03-01 - 2027-03-31
 - Platform Fee (Private License): $30,000
 - Platform Fee (Committed): $30,000
 - Reserved Capacity: $90,000
 - Support (Standard): $12,000
 - Migration reversal credit: -$10,000
Total: $152,000
```

Samir Patel: Looks like both the private license SKU and the old committed SKU were billed for the same period. Contract AMND-1458 shows they migrated to private license effective 2027-03-01, so only one platform fee should apply. Also they upgraded support to Enterprise effective 2027-02-10, so Standard is wrong.

Support Billing Bot: Invoice INV-2027-0223 generated by billing-pipeline v3.5.0. Automated check: duplicate product_code detected; support-tier mismatch flagged.

Priya Natarajan: Quick math needed for procurement: how much to credit?

Lena Ortiz: Remove duplicate platform line = credit $30,000. Support delta: Enterprise monthly $18,000 vs Standard $12,000 => difference $6,000 underbilled (we owe invoice increase). Migration reversal credit -$10,000 should not be on renewal per AMND-1458 (it applied only once in Feb). So removing that credit increases net owed by $10,000. Net customer-facing change = -30,000 (credit) +6,000 (charge) +10,000 (remove credit) = -14,000 -> we owe customer $14,000.

Samir Patel: Agreed. Recommend issuing credit memo CM-2027-089 for $14,000 and reissuing INV-2027-0223R without the duplicate platform line and with Enterprise support applied. Keep wording neutral.

Eng Billing: Opening ticket BILL-10110 to fix join logic that allowed historical committed SKU to persist after migration; add test to ensure one platform fee per active license at term start.

Support Billing Bot: Ticket BILL-10110 created: "duplicate platform fee after license migration + stale migration credit reapplied". Status: triaged.

Finance Ops: Will prepare CM and reissued invoice once you confirm final numbers. Please confirm we owe customer $14,000 credit.

Priya Natarajan: Confirmed $14,000 credit. Draft customer message: "Hi <AP Contact>, we identified an inconsistency on invoice INV-2027-0223 and have issued credit memo CM-2027-089 for $14,000. A corrected invoice (INV-2027-0223R) has been posted to the portal. We apologize for the disruption and are available to help with any procurement questions."

Lena Ortiz: Suggest adding contacts for procurement follow-up: Samir and Priya. Also attach AMND-1458 excerpt.

Finance Ops: Created CM-2027-089 for $14,000 and reissued INV-2027-0223R with corrected lines: Platform Fee (Private License) $30,000, Reserved Capacity $90,000, Support (Enterprise) $18,000. Corrected total: $138,000 (CM applied net due $124,000). Uploaded docs to portal and emailed procurement per neutral wording.

Support Billing Bot: BILL-10110 assigned to eng-billing; PR opened to prevent duplicate SKUs.

Samir Patel: Procurement confirmed receipt and will proceed with PO. Finance Ops, please post journal entries and revenue true-up.

Finance Ops: Journal entries posted, credit applied. Marking thread resolved.

Priya Natarajan: Thanks all. Follow-up: Eng Billing, please track post-deploy incidents on billing-pipeline to ensure migratory cases are clean.

Eng Billing: Will monitor; adding post-deploy check to BILL-10110.

Support Billing Bot: INV-2027-0223 status: corrected (INV-2027-0223R). CM-2027-089 applied. BILL-10110 in progress.

title: #eng-platform: 1769436677
preview:
Nadia Rahman: hey folks — working through alerting for the marketplace entitlement sync job (post PR-19878). can we align on thresholds + what should page vs just warn? also need a quick dashboard spec for partnerships/support.

Cole Summers: yep. we should separate “job is broken” from “external propagation is slow”. job-broken should page.

Ethan Park: +1. i’d page on: no successful syncs for X mins, or sync loop crashing, or backlog not draining.

Nadia Rahman: current metrics we have in staging:
- `entitlement_sync_run_success_total{provider}` / `..._failure_total{provider,reason}`
- `entitlement_sync_loop_duration_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_sync_backlog_items{provider}` (queue length)
- `entitlement_sync_oldest_item_age_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_sync_last_success_timestamp_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_provisioning_time_seconds{provider}` (subscription seen -> org entitled)

Cole Summers: nice. proposal:
P0/page:
1) `time() - last_success > 10m` for any provider (aws or gcp)
2) backlog age > 20m AND backlog > 0 (means we’re not draining)
3) failure ratio > 50% for 5m (likely bad creds / schema mismatch)

Priya Natarajan: 10m might be a little aggressive if we pause deploys / incident, but for marketplace onboarding it’s probably right. what’s current cron cadence?

Ethan Park: prod job runs every 60s. if we miss 10 consecutive runs that’s usually “something is wrong” (crashloop, db lock, provider 5xx + no backoff handling).

Chloe Martin: from console side: if entitlement isn’t visible within ~5–10 min, customers hit “retry” a bunch and open tickets. so paging at 10m seems fine.

Leila Farouk: can we add a burn-rate style alert too? like “oldest item age p95 > 10m for 15m”? reduces flapping.

Nadia Rahman: yep — we can do multi-window. ex:
- page if `oldest_item_age > 20m` for 5m
- warn if `oldest_item_age > 10m` for 15m

Cole Summers: + warn on spikes in `reason=dimension_mapping_error` specifically. that was the SUP-4829 pain.

Ethan Park: agree. the mapping errors are actionable + almost always our fault.

Nadia Rahman: ok, so alert taxonomy:
P0 (PagerDuty):
- No successful sync for provider in 10m
- Oldest backlog item age > 20m for 5m
- CrashLooping / job not scheduled (can we detect via heartbeat?)
P1 (Slack #eng-platform / #marketplace-escalations):
- Oldest backlog item age > 10m for 15m
- Failure ratio > 10% for 15m
- Any `dimension_mapping_error` > 0 for 5m

Priya Natarajan: for heartbeat, simplest is a gauge updated each loop: `entitlement_sync_heartbeat_timestamp_seconds{provider}`. then alert on `time() - heartbeat > 5m`.

Cole Summers: +1. last_success can be “no work to do” and not update. heartbeat confirms job alive.

Nadia Rahman: good call — last_success is only emitted on successful write. we’ve had “alive but nothing processed” cases.

Dev Patel: support angle: please route P1 to a channel we watch. #marketplace-escalations is best. and add a runbook link w/ “what to tell customer” (expected propagation delay, where to check org status).

Chloe Martin: + include console error code mapping in runbook. if alert is `dimension_mapping_error`, support should ask for `x-redwood-request-id` and org id.

Ethan Park: dashboard panels i’d like:
1) Backlog: `entitlement_sync_backlog_items` stacked by provider
2) Oldest age: `entitlement_sync_oldest_item_age_seconds` (max + p95)
3) Success/fail rate: `rate(success_total[5m])` vs `rate(failure_total[5m])`
4) Failures by reason (top 5)
5) Loop duration p50/p95 (so we catch DB slowness)
6) Provisioning time p50/p95 (subscription->entitled)

Nadia Rahman: yup. and annotate deploys.

Cole Summers: also split by env? at least prod vs staging selector.

Nadia Rahman: dashboard will default prod with templated `env` + `provider`. i’ll add a panel for “entitlement verification API error rate” too (console depends on it).

Priya Natarajan: don’t page on provisioning_time directly — that includes external propagation. but it’s useful as a KPI panel.

Leila Farouk: +1. keep provisioning_time as dashboard + weekly report, not paging.

Nadia Rahman: ack. so provisioning_time: KPI only, maybe a slack digest.

Cole Summers: what about provider outages (aws marketplace api 5xx)? should we page?

Ethan Park: if it causes backlog age > 20m, yes. but alert should mention “provider_errors”. we can tag reasons: `provider_http_5xx`, `provider_timeout`.

Nadia Rahman: we already label `reason` w/ `aws_5xx`, `aws_throttle`, `gcp_5xx`, etc. i’ll make those show up in “failures by reason”.

PagerDuty: [Auto-configuration] Service “marketplace-entitlement-sync” has an existing alert rule “High error rate” (disabled). Owner: Platform.

Priya Natarajan: lol. re-enable but with the new rules + proper routing.

Dev Patel: please also add a short “expected behavior” section to runbook: entitlement propagation can take up to ~15 min on rare cases; tell customer we’re monitoring + can manually re-sync if needed.

Cole Summers: manual re-sync command still exists? `entitlementsync backfill --org <id> --provider aws`?

Ethan Park: yep, but gated. runbook should say “eng-only”.

Nadia Rahman: action items:
- Nadia: add heartbeat metric + alerts (P0/P1) + create Grafana dashboard (prod/staging templates)
- Cole: confirm failure reasons we should treat as “our fault” vs provider
- Chloe/Dev: provide runbook snippets + support messaging

Cole Summers: sounds good. i’ll drop the “our fault” list here:
- `dimension_mapping_error`
- `schema_validation_error`
- `signature_invalid` (our creds/config)
- `db_write_failed`
Provider-ish:

Nadia Rahman: hey folks — working through alerting for the marketplace entitlement sync job (post PR-19878). can we align on thresholds + what should page vs just warn? also need a quick dashboard spec for partnerships/support.

Cole Summers: yep. we should separate “job is broken” from “external propagation is slow”. job-broken should page.

Ethan Park: +1. i’d page on: no successful syncs for X mins, or sync loop crashing, or backlog not draining.

Nadia Rahman: current metrics we have in staging:
- `entitlement_sync_run_success_total{provider}` / `..._failure_total{provider,reason}`
- `entitlement_sync_loop_duration_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_sync_backlog_items{provider}` (queue length)
- `entitlement_sync_oldest_item_age_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_sync_last_success_timestamp_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_provisioning_time_seconds{provider}` (subscription seen -> org entitled)

Cole Summers: nice. proposal:
P0/page:
1) `time() - last_success > 10m` for any provider (aws or gcp)
2) backlog age > 20m AND backlog > 0 (means we’re not draining)
3) failure ratio > 50% for 5m (likely bad creds / schema mismatch)

Priya Natarajan: 10m might be a little aggressive if we pause deploys / incident, but for marketplace onboarding it’s probably right. what’s current cron cadence?

Ethan Park: prod job runs every 60s. if we miss 10 consecutive runs that’s usually “something is wrong” (crashloop, db lock, provider 5xx + no backoff handling).

Chloe Martin: from console side: if entitlement isn’t visible within ~5–10 min, customers hit “retry” a bunch and open tickets. so paging at 10m seems fine.

Leila Farouk: can we add a burn-rate style alert too? like “oldest item age p95 > 10m for 15m”? reduces flapping.

Nadia Rahman: yep — we can do multi-window. ex:
- page if `oldest_item_age > 20m` for 5m
- warn if `oldest_item_age > 10m` for 15m

Cole Summers: + warn on spikes in `reason=dimension_mapping_error` specifically. that was the SUP-4829 pain.

Ethan Park: agree. the mapping errors are actionable + almost always our fault.

Nadia Rahman: ok, so alert taxonomy:
P0 (PagerDuty):
- No successful sync for provider in 10m
- Oldest backlog item age > 20m for 5m
- CrashLooping / job not scheduled (can we detect via heartbeat?)
P1 (Slack #eng-platform / #marketplace-escalations):
- Oldest backlog item age > 10m for 15m
- Failure ratio > 10% for 15m
- Any `dimension_mapping_error` > 0 for 5m

Priya Natarajan: for heartbeat, simplest is a gauge updated each loop: `entitlement_sync_heartbeat_timestamp_seconds{provider}`. then alert on `time() - heartbeat > 5m`.

Cole Summers: +1. last_success can be “no work to do” and not update. heartbeat confirms job alive.

Nadia Rahman: good call — last_success is only emitted on successful write. we’ve had “alive but nothing processed” cases.

Dev Patel: support angle: please route P1 to a channel we watch. #marketplace-escalations is best. and add a runbook link w/ “what to tell customer” (expected propagation delay, where to check org status).

Chloe Martin: + include console error code mapping in runbook. if alert is `dimension_mapping_error`, support should ask for `x-redwood-request-id` and org id.

Ethan Park: dashboard panels i’d like:
1) Backlog: `entitlement_sync_backlog_items` stacked by provider
2) Oldest age: `entitlement_sync_oldest_item_age_seconds` (max + p95)
3) Success/fail rate: `rate(success_total[5m])` vs `rate(failure_total[5m])`
4) Failures by reason (top 5)
5) Loop duration p50/p95 (so we catch DB slowness)
6) Provisioning time p50/p95 (subscription->entitled)

Nadia Rahman: yup. and annotate deploys.

Cole Summers: also split by env? at least prod vs staging selector.

Nadia Rahman: dashboard will default prod with templated `env` + `provider`. i’ll add a panel for “entitlement verification API error rate” too (console depends on it).

Priya Natarajan: don’t page on provisioning_time directly — that includes external propagation. but it’s useful as a KPI panel.

Leila Farouk: +1. keep provisioning_time as dashboard + weekly report, not paging.

Nadia Rahman: ack. so provisioning_time: KPI only, maybe a slack digest.

Cole Summers: what about provider outages (aws marketplace api 5xx)? should we page?

Ethan Park: if it causes backlog age > 20m, yes. but alert should mention “provider_errors”. we can tag reasons: `provider_http_5xx`, `provider_timeout`.

Nadia Rahman: we already label `reason` w/ `aws_5xx`, `aws_throttle`, `gcp_5xx`, etc. i’ll make those show up in “failures by reason”.

PagerDuty: [Auto-configuration] Service “marketplace-entitlement-sync” has an existing alert rule “High error rate” (disabled). Owner: Platform.

Priya Natarajan: lol. re-enable but with the new rules + proper routing.

Dev Patel: please also add a short “expected behavior” section to runbook: entitlement propagation can take up to ~15 min on rare cases; tell customer we’re monitoring + can manually re-sync if needed.

Cole Summers: manual re-sync command still exists? `entitlementsync backfill --org <id> --provider aws`?

Ethan Park: yep, but gated. runbook should say “eng-only”.

Nadia Rahman: action items:
- Nadia: add heartbeat metric + alerts (P0/P1) + create Grafana dashboard (prod/staging templates)
- Cole: confirm failure reasons we should treat as “our fault” vs provider
- Chloe/Dev: provide runbook snippets + support messaging

Cole Summers: sounds good. i’ll drop the “our fault” list here:
- `dimension_mapping_error`
- `schema_validation_error`
- `signature_invalid` (our creds/config)
- `db_write_failed`

title: #eng-security: 1769401188
preview:
Marcus Lin: Dropping proposed marketplace security claims here for final accuracy check before Docs publishes + PMM puts into AWS/GCP listing. Please nitpick wording.

Claims (draft):
1) “All customer data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES‑256).”
2) “Customer content (prompts/outputs) is not used to train models.”
3) “Hosted API request/response payloads are retained for 30 days by default for abuse monitoring and debugging; customers can request shorter retention.”
4) “Audit logs are available for admin actions and API key events, and can be exported to customer SIEM.”
5) “Keys are managed via cloud KMS; Dedicated/Private customers can use customer-managed keys.”
6) “Data residency: processing occurs in the region selected by the customer (where available).”

Need to confirm what’s true across Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private, and what needs qualifiers.

Naomi Feldman: :eyes: going through one by one. Overall ok but some are over-broad. We need product-mode qualifiers or AWS/GCP reviewers will bounce it.

Naomi Feldman: 1) “AES‑256 at rest” is usually fine but we should say “encrypted at rest using industry-standard encryption” unless we can prove every storage layer is AES‑256 (object store, DB, backups, logs). Also “TLS 1.2+” ok, but note some internal service-to-service is mTLS—don’t overspec.

Olga Petrov: +1. From compliance standpoint, over-specific crypto claims create evidence burden. Better: “encrypted at rest and in transit” and keep TLS 1.2+ if we have a standard.

Marcus Lin: Got it. We can change to “TLS 1.2+” + “encryption at rest” without naming AES. Or “AES‑256 where supported by underlying cloud services”?

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Don’t do “where supported” — reads weak. Prefer: “Data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest using industry-standard encryption. Encryption is provided by major cloud provider managed services for Hosted.”

Elliot Price: For docs, I can implement mode-specific callouts: Hosted / Dedicated / Private. Listing copy is tighter though. We can keep listing generic and link to full security page.

Alex Martinez: +1, marketplace pages can be brief and point to the “Marketplace Security Overview” doc. But the claims on listing still need to be defensible.

Marcus Lin: 2) “not used to train models” — true for customer content? I know we don’t train foundation models, but do we use any prompts/outputs for eval/quality monitoring if customer opts in?

Naomi Feldman: We have an opt-in program for sharing data for quality improvements (explicit). Default is no. Wording should be: “We do not use customer content to train Redwood or third-party foundation models without explicit customer permission.”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Yes. Avoid absolute “never” statements. Add “without explicit permission” / “unless customer opts in.”

Daniel Carter: From evidence POV, we can point to policy + DPA language. But the marketing claim should match the policy exactly.

Marcus Lin: 3) Retention: “30 days by default” — is that accurate for Hosted logs? I recall payload retention is shorter in prod unless debug is enabled.

Priya Natarajan: Looping in (platform). For Hosted: request metadata (timings, token counts, error codes) retained longer for billing/analytics. Full payload retention is *not* 30 days universally. We store payloads only for a subset of traffic for abuse detection + customer support debugging, and it’s configurable per tenant. Default is currently 7 days for payload sampling, 30 days for derived logs/metrics. (reaction: :warning: x3)

Naomi Feldman: This is exactly why we need to split “payload” vs “metadata.” Marketplace reviewers also ask specifically about “customer content.” Payload == customer content.

Marcus Lin: Ok so claim 3 as written is wrong. Proposed rewrite:
- “By default, Redwood retains limited request metadata for billing and operational monitoring. Customer content (prompt/response payloads) is not retained except as necessary for abuse prevention and support, and retention can be configured by plan.”

Alex Martinez: That’s closer. We should also provide a concrete number somewhere in the security doc, but maybe not in the listing. Buyers ask “how many days” though.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Put the exact defaults in the security packet (versioned) and keep listing language high-level. If defaults change, we don’t want to update marketplace listings every time.

Olga Petrov: +1. Also, ensure we mention “deletion within X days of retention expiration” if we have that in policy.

Marcus Lin: 4) Audit logs: We definitely log admin actions + API key events in console. Do we have guaranteed export to SIEM today or is it “available via API” and customer builds integration?

Priya Natarajan: Audit log export: currently available via API + S3 sink for Dedicated (feature flag). Hosted has API access; SIEM integrations are “customer managed” (Splunk, etc.) via webhook/collector on their side. No turnkey “one-click Splunk app.”

Elliot Price: So the claim should be: “Audit logs can be accessed via the Redwood Console and API; export options available (plan-dependent).”

Naomi Feldman: Yes, and list events: “org membership changes, role changes, API key create/revoke, policy changes, model deployment/rollout actions.” Don’t claim “all events.”

Marcus Lin: 5) KMS + CMK: “Keys are managed via cloud KMS; Dedicated/Private can use customer-managed keys.” True for Dedicated? I think we support CMK for certain storage only.

Priya Natarajan: Dedicated: CMK supported for object storage + log storage when deployed in customer VPC / dedicated environment, not for every internal service. Private (customer-controlled) can integrate with customer KMS/HSM depending on deployment.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Wording suggestion:
- “Hosted: encryption keys are managed by Redwood using cloud-provider key management services.”
- “Dedicated/Private: customer-managed keys are supported for select storage systems; details available in security packet.”

Olga Petrov: “select storage systems” is safe. Also avoid “HSM” unless we can deliver it across clouds consistently.

Marcus Lin: 6) Data residency: “processing occurs in region selected” — but fallback routing could cross-region if customer enables it, right?

Naomi Feldman: Yes. Also Dedicated/Private are pinned, Hosted has region choice but some control-plane metadata might be global.

Priya Natarajan: Correct. In Hosted, control plane is multi-region but some auth/billing metadata replicated. We do not move payload cross-region unless customer has multi-region failover enabled. Need that nuance.

Alex Martinez: Suggest listing language:
- “Hosted supports region selection. Data is processed in-region by default. Optional multi-region failover can be enabled.”
Then security doc has the full “what is in-region vs global metadata.”

Marcus Lin: Summarizing edits we’ll apply:
- Remove AES‑256 mention; keep “encrypted at rest” + “TLS 1.2+”
- Training: add “without explicit permission/opt-in”
- Retention: remove “30 days payload” claim; split metadata vs content; move concrete defaults to security packet
- Audit logs: “available in console + API; export options plan-dependent; event types listed; not ‘all events’”
- KMS/CMK: qualify to Hosted vs Dedicated/Private + “select systems”
- Residency: “processed in-region by default; note control-plane metadata + optional failover”

Dr. Aisha Rahman: Approved direction. Please also add “shared responsibility” sentence somewhere (marketplace reviewers expect it). Something like “Redwood secures the platform; customers are responsible for securing their accounts, API keys, and client-side usage.”

Olga Petrov: And ensure the security overview references SOC 2 Type II coverage without implying certification scope beyond what we have. Use “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA/on request.”

Elliot Price: I’ll update the docs PR copy accordingly and tag @Marcus Lin @Naomi Feldman for final pass. Also will add a “Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private” table so we stop mixing claims.

Alex Martinez: Once wording is updated, please paste the final “approved marketplace-safe copy block” back here so PMM can lift it directly.

Marcus Lin: Will do. I’ll post the revised block later today after I integrate Priya’s notes on retention + residency.

Priya Natarajan: If helpful I can drop exact current defaults for (a) payload sampling retention (b) metrics retention (c) audit log retention per plan. But agree—keep it in packet, not listing.

Naomi Feldman: :thumbsup: Also please sanity check that “TLS 1.2+” matches our edge config everywhere (incl. older SDKs). If any endpoints are still negotiating 1.2 only, still fine, but don’t claim 1.3.

Marcus Lin: Ack. I’ll verify with edge config and keep it “TLS 1.2+” only.

Daniel Carter: When you update the claim block, please ensure we link to the evidence index page, not directly to internal Jira/Confluence. Marketplace reviewers will need something shareable.

Marcus Lin: Yep. Thanks all. (reaction: :white_check_mark: x4)

preview:
Confluence Bot: ADR updated: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-022+Cost+Breakdown+Taxonomy (page changed by Ethan Park)

Ethan Park: hey folks, last call on ADR-022 wording. biggest open item is the “overhead allocation” definition + how it shows up in breakdown. pls drop any final comments here so i can merge today.

Allison Grant: skimmed. taxonomy is good. one thing: the ADR currently uses “overhead” in 2 ways (platform shared + request-level overhead like queueing). recommend we reserve “overhead” for *shared/non-attributable* and call the other “request_execution_overhead” or similar.

Logan Wright: +1. telemetry already has “queue_time_ms” etc, we don’t want that to get lumped into Finance overhead.

Laura Bennett: yes, please do not label queueing/runtime inefficiencies as “overhead” in finance sense. overhead for FP&A is “shared costs not attributable to a single request” (control plane, idle reserve, platform ops, certain committed capacity deltas depending on contract structure).

Rishi Malhotra: also need a clear statement re: when overhead is applied.
- Console: mostly wants per-request attributable cost + transparent line item for “allocated overhead (optional)”
- Finance reporting: we need fully-loaded numbers for unit economics rollups.
If ADR can explicitly allow both views, that helps.

Marissa Cole: from Console POV: if we show overhead, it has to be clearly labeled as allocation + have a link/tooltip. otherwise we’ll get “why doesn’t this match invoice” tickets again.

Cole Summers: billing hooks angle: invoices will remain source of truth for “amount billed” (pricing, discounts, credits). cost model is “cost of serving.” we can align primitives but we should be explicit in ADR that overhead allocation is not used for billing.

Ethan Park: makes sense. here’s proposed revised wording for ADR section 3.2; pls nit:

Ethan Park: 
"Overhead (allocated) represents shared, non-request-attributable platform costs that are apportioned across requests for internal unit-economics analysis (e.g., baseline idle capacity, fleet management, control-plane operations). Overhead is NOT used for invoicing and MUST be explicitly marked as an allocation.

The cost model MUST produce (a) request-attributable cost and (b) optionally, fully-loaded cost that includes allocated overhead, gated by an overhead allocation policy.

Any per-request runtime inefficiency (e.g., queue time, scheduling delays) remains part of request-attributable execution cost and MUST NOT be labeled as overhead." 

Rafael Mendes: looks good. SRE nit: define the policy id as stable + versioned; we need to know what policy was applied when we investigate drift.

Allison Grant: +1. add something like `overhead_allocation_policy_id` and “if overhead isn’t applied, this field is empty.” determinism requirement.

Nadia Rahman: can we also say something about “overhead may be 0 for a given deployment context”? e.g., customer-private/on-prem where we don’t have visibility or we intentionally don’t allocate shared costs.

Laura Bennett: yes. for Private, overhead allocation may be N/A depending on contract and data availability. important that absence != zero. we should encode “not applied” vs “applied with 0 value.”

Ethan Park: ok. proposing fields in the semantics text (not schema):
- `overhead_allocated_usd` (or local currency but we’re USD internally)
- `overhead_allocation_applied` boolean
- `overhead_allocation_policy_id` string
And explicit: if `applied=false` then amount is omitted (not 0).

Rishi Malhotra: minor wording: “apportioned across requests” -> “allocated across requests via a documented policy” (sounds less arbitrary)

Cole Summers: also call out that policy can differ by deployment mode (hosted/dedicated/private) + reporting window.

Marissa Cole: re UI: can we use “Allocated overhead” as the label in the ADR? that’s what we’ll mirror in Console.

Ethan Park: yep. will standardize on “Allocated overhead”.

Priya Natarajan: this is in good shape. just ensure the ADR says (1) request-attributable cost is the default for product surfaces, (2) fully-loaded is opt-in, policy-driven. 

Allison Grant: +1. also add a one-liner that breakdown totals must always equal the sum of components, with overhead included only when applied.

Ethan Park: ack. final patch:
- rename runtime “overhead” wording to “execution overhead” where needed
- add “Allocated overhead” definition above
- add policy fields + determinism note
- add “not applied vs zero” note

Laura Bennett: works for Finance. thanks.

Rishi Malhotra: +1.

Logan Wright: +1.

Ethan Park: cool. merging ADR changes in ~30m unless new objections.

Confluence Bot: Ethan Park published updates to https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-022+Cost+Breakdown+Taxonomy

Marissa Cole: reacted with :thumbsup:

Rafael Mendes: reacted with :white_check_mark:

Rishi Malhotra: also need a clear statement re: when overhead is applied.
- Console: mostly wants per-request attributable cost + transparent line item for “allocated overhead (optional)”
- Finance reporting: we need fully-loaded numbers for unit economics rollups.
If ADR can explicitly allow both views, that helps.

Marissa Cole: from Console POV: if we show overhead, it has to be clearly labeled as allocation + have a link/tooltip. otherwise we’ll get “why doesn’t this match invoice” tickets again.

Cole Summers: billing hooks angle: invoices will remain source of truth for “amount billed” (pricing, discounts, credits). cost model is “cost of serving.” we can align primitives but we should be explicit in ADR that overhead allocation is not used for billing.

Ethan Park: makes sense. here’s proposed revised wording for ADR section 3.2; pls nit:

Ethan Park: 
"Overhead (allocated) represents shared, non-request-attributable platform costs that are apportioned across requests for internal unit-economics analysis (e.g., baseline idle capacity, fleet management, control-plane operations). Overhead is NOT used for invoicing and MUST be explicitly marked as an allocation.

The cost model MUST produce (a) request-attributable cost and (b) optionally, fully-loaded cost that includes allocated overhead, gated by an overhead allocation policy.

Any per-request runtime inefficiency (e.g., queue time, scheduling delays) remains part of request-attributable execution cost and MUST NOT be labeled as overhead." 

Rafael Mendes: looks good. SRE nit: define the policy id as stable + versioned; we need to know what policy was applied when we investigate drift.

Allison Grant: +1. add something like `overhead_allocation_policy_id` and “if overhead isn’t applied, this field is empty.” determinism requirement.

Nadia Rahman: can we also say something about “overhead may be 0 for a given deployment context”? e.g., customer-private/on-prem where we don’t have visibility or we intentionally don’t allocate shared costs.

Laura Bennett: yes. for Private, overhead allocation may be N/A depending on contract and data availability. important that absence != zero. we should encode “not applied” vs “applied with 0 value.”

Ethan Park: ok. proposing fields in the semantics text (not schema):
- `overhead_allocated_usd` (or local currency but we’re USD internally)
- `overhead_allocation_applied` boolean
- `overhead_allocation_policy_id` string
And explicit: if `applied=false` then amount is omitted (not 0).

Rishi Malhotra: minor wording: “apportioned across requests” -> “allocated across requests via a documented policy” (sounds less arbitrary)

Cole Summers: also call out that policy can differ by deployment mode (hosted/dedicated/private) + reporting window.

Marissa Cole: re UI: can we use “Allocated overhead” as the label in the ADR? that’s what we’ll mirror in Console.

Ethan Park: yep. will standardize on “Allocated overhead”.

Priya Natarajan: this is in good shape. just ensure the ADR says (1) request-attributable cost is the default for product surfaces, (2) fully-loaded is opt-in, policy-driven. 

Allison Grant: +1. also add a one-liner that breakdown totals must always equal the sum of components, with overhead included only when applied.

Ethan Park: ack. final patch:
- rename runtime “overhead” wording to “execution overhead” where needed
- add “Allocated overhead” definition above
- add policy fields + determinism note
- add “not applied vs zero” note

Laura Bennett: works for Finance. thanks.

Rishi Malhotra: +1.

Logan Wright: +1.

Ethan Park: cool. merging ADR changes in ~30m unless new objections.
